mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is rising while FEDFUNDS remains low, real policy rates stay compressed and the market prices less immediate tightening. That combination supports equity multiples and predicts a + direction for the 10-year yield over the next 20 trading days through higher inflation compensation and lower duration demand.
failure_condition: Reject if CPIAUCSL rises for the next two monthly prints while the 10-year yield falls or stays flat in more than 60% of triggered 20-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals softer labor demand and slower nominal growth. That weakens inflation and growth expectations, so the 10-year yield should move lower over the next 20 trading days after the labor cooling threshold is crossed.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points versus the prior 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE-triggered episodes are followed by higher 10-year yields in more than 60% of cases over a 20-trading-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous strengthening in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicates broadening final demand and labor income growth. That combination raises expected real activity and nominal bond supply pressure, pushing the 10-year yield higher over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint RSAFS-PAYEMS strength does not precede higher 10-year yields in at least 55% of 5-day windows, or if the effect disappears when either series is strong alone.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO accelerates after a weak patch, markets infer improving industrial capacity utilization and firmer real growth. That lifts term premium and pushes the 10-year yield upward over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change turns positive after being negative for at least 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if positive-turning INDPRO episodes are not followed by rising 10-year yields more often than not, or if the yield response is negative on average.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST signals weaker housing activity and lower private domestic demand. That reduces near-term growth expectations and should pull the 10-year yield down over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 5% versus its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside shocks are followed by higher 10-year yields in more than 60% of triggered 20-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in FEDFUNDS above the market's prior setting tightens financial conditions and raises short-end discount rates. Over the next 60 trading days this should drag the 10-year yield lower as growth expectations soften and bond duration becomes more attractive.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by at least 0.50 percentage points from its level 3 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if sizable FEDFUNDS hikes are followed by higher 10-year yields or if the average 60-day yield change is nonnegative after tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A joint slowdown in GDPC1 and RSAFS identifies a demand downshift that is stronger than either series alone. When both weaken together, the 10-year yield should fall over the next 60 trading days because investors price lower nominal growth and lower inflation pass-through.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous GDPC1 and RSAFS weakening does not precede lower 10-year yields in at least 60% of 60-day windows, or if either proxy alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: In a cooling labor market, a low and stable UNRATE combined with continued PAYEMS gains signals resilient wage income and sticky domestic demand. That regime should support a higher 10-year yield over the next 20 trading days via firmer growth expectations and reduced recession pricing.
failure_condition: Reject if low-UNRATE plus positive-PAYEMS episodes fail to produce higher 10-year yields over 20 trading days, or if the sign flips when the same labor pattern repeats.
